name: Pan-Fried Catfish
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the beans under cold running water, then place them in a saucepan with the sherry. Place over medium heat and cook for 2 minutes.
2. Add the fish stock, garlic, and ginger to the saucepan. Cook for another 5 minutes, reducing by about one-third.
3. Scrape the liquid and beans into a blender and puree. Pass the puree through a strainer to remove the skins of the beans and return it to the saucepan.
4. Add the cream and cook until the sauce has a consistency that coats the back of a spoon. Turn off the heat, then whisk in the butter. Keep the sauce warm while you cook the fish.
5. Dry the catfish fillets on towels and dredge lightly in cornstarch, shaking off the excess.
6. Heat the vegetable oil in a skillet until hot. Place the catfish fillets in the hot oil. Cook for 3 to 4 minutes on each side, or until crispy and dark golden in color.
7. Remove the fillets to paper towels and pat dry.
8. To serve, drizzle a little sauce at the bottom of the serving platter. Arrange the fillets on top and decorate with cilantro. Offer additional sauce on the side.
